rounds.agent_message: Seed round copied from the highest existing source round.

Implemented the MFMA-based depthwise conv kernel in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p85/optimization_rounds/round1/output_model_new.py), preserving the fixed `128x128` block tile, fixed `2x2` warp grid, fixed `32x32x8` BF16 MFMA fragments, and the required accumulator writeback mapping. The optimized path is cudagraph-safe for weight metadata/tensors via `_get_cached_weight`, with no convolution fallback in `forward()`. I also updated the listed callsites to this round: [test_conv2d_asym.py](/workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py) [bench_conv2d.py](/workspace/substrate/benchmark/conv2d/bench_conv2d.py) The required case marker was written at [case.txt](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p85/optimization_rounds/round1/path/to/case.txt), and `python -m pytest /workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py` passed with `2 passed`.

Implemented the split-K round2 kernel in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p85/optimization_rounds/round2/output_model_new.py). It keeps the `128 x 128` MFMA tile and fixed lane/accumulator mapping, launches `SPLIT_K_SLICES = 2`, atomically reduces per-split fp32 partials into an fp32 workspace, then runs a separate finalize kernel to write bf16 NCHW output. I also kept weight caching and added cached workspace/output buffers for the optimized path. I updated the round selectors in [test_conv2d_asym.py](/workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py) and [bench_conv2d.py](/workspace/substrate/benchmark/conv2d/bench_conv2d.py), and wrote [case.txt](/workspace/kernel_benchmark/path/to/case.txt). Validation: `python -m pytest /workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py` passed with `2 passed`.
